hidden_state.scenario_summary: Synthetic clean covered outage where a covered service exceeds the credit threshold and clearly qualifies for a contract remedy.
latent_facts: The incident is customer-visible and on a covered service.
The outage duration clears the threshold for a flat credit.
The case should not require extra review in v1.
likely_failure_modes: Model under-calls credit eligibility despite clear policy coverage.
Model routes exclusively to engineering and misses the shared commercial response.

case_packet.title: Synthetic clean covered outage 20

notes: Premium SLA addendum includes flat credit for covered outages over 30 minutes.

usage_record.telemetry_summary: Usage outside the outage window was normal.

incident_record.engineering_summary: Incident review points to scheduler failure; the service remained fully unavailable during the covered window.

case_packet.customer_note: Our team saw a sustained outage on the covered service and wants the incident and remedy confirmed.
case_packet.policy_snippet: Covered outages longer than 30 minutes on premium managed services receive a flat service credit.
case_packet.calculator_output: Covered outage duration: 74 minutes. Credit threshold met.
case_packet.visible_case_summary: Synthetic clean covered outage with explicit credit eligibility.

adjudication_notes: This family is meant to be a clean positive-control outage case.
ground_truth.reference_customer_note: We confirmed a covered outage longer than the contract threshold, so the flat service credit applies. We will share the incident summary and confirm the credit handling in the billing workflow.
ground_truth.reference_internal_note: Owner: shared_revops_engineering. Action: send_explanation_only because the outage was covered, exceeded threshold, and qualifies for credit_due.
